CHROME WEB STORE SUBMISSION GUIDE
===================================

LISTING TAB
-----------

Name: Ibid — Citation Manager

Short Description (132 chars max):
Privacy-first citation manager. One-click citations in APA, MLA, Chicago, Harvard & 2,600+ styles. No account needed.

Category: Productivity > Tools

Language: English (United States)

Detailed Description:

Ibid — from Latin "ibidem," meaning "in the same place" — is a citation manager built for students, researchers, and academics who need accurate references without compromising their privacy.

Ibid runs a full Citation Style Language (CSL) engine directly in your browser using WebAssembly. All citation processing happens locally on your device. No data is sent to external servers. No account is required.

HOW IT WORKS

1. Visit any webpage, journal article, or book page
2. Click the Ibid icon — metadata is extracted automatically
3. Choose your citation style and copy the formatted reference

Ibid supports one-click citing from academic publishers, news sites, and general webpages.

CITATION STYLES

74 styles bundled offline, including APA, MLA, Chicago, Harvard, and IEEE. 2,600+ additional styles from the official CSL repository can be downloaded on demand. Once downloaded, they work offline.

FEATURES

Citation & Formatting
  - Accurate bibliography and in-text citations
  - Parenthetical and narrative in-text toggle
  - Smart metadata extraction from multiple web standards (structured data, meta tags, and page content)
  - PDF metadata extraction via Rust/WASM — reads embedded text, document properties, and identifier patterns
  - Editable fields — review and correct any auto-detected data before saving
  - Auto-enhance — paste a DOI, ISBN, or other identifier to fill all fields from public academic APIs
  - Multi-source resolver with automatic fallback for reliable results
  - Title-based search fallback when no identifier is found

Library Management
  - Organize citations into projects
  - Star, tag, and search your references
  - Inline citation preview with per-entry style selection
  - Duplicate detection
  - Add entries manually with a built-in form
  - Side panel stays open while you research

Import & Export
  - Import and export in common academic formats (BibTeX, RIS, CSL-JSON, and more)
  - Filter exports by project, type, or starred status

Additional
  - Dark mode
  - Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Shift+C to cite)
  - Context menu integration
  - Session persistence — reopen the popup without losing your work

PRIVACY

  - All citation processing happens locally in your browser
  - No telemetry, analytics, or tracking
  - No data collection — your library stays on your device
  - Network requests only for identifier lookups and optional style downloads
  - Minimal required permissions — no broad host access
  - Optional scholarly API access — requested only when needed, you choose whether to grant it
  - Full privacy policy: https://ibid.tools/privacy

TECHNICAL DETAILS

Ibid uses a Rust/WebAssembly citation engine powered by Hayagriva (the CSL processor from the Typst project) for accurate rendering of all 2,600+ official CSL styles. An embedded English locale ensures correct terms ("et al.," month names, ordinals). The WASM engine also handles BibTeX and RIS parsing for fast import and export.

For questions, feedback, or bug reports:
  Website: https://ibid.tools
  GitHub: https://github.com/oriclabs/ibid
  Support: support@ibid.tools
  Privacy Policy: https://ibid.tools/privacy

PRIVACY TAB
-----------

Privacy Policy URL: https://ibid.tools/privacy

Single purpose description:
Ibid is a citation manager that extracts bibliographic metadata from webpages and formats citations in academic styles (APA, MLA, Chicago, etc.). It runs entirely in the browser using WebAssembly.

Data usage:
  - Does NOT collect: personally identifiable information, health information, financial information, authentication information, personal communications, location, web history, user activity, website content
  - Does NOT sell user data
  - Does NOT use data for purposes unrelated to the extension's functionality
  - Does NOT use data for creditworthiness or lending purposes

Permission justifications (copy these into the form fields):

activeTab:
Ibid needs to read the current page's metadata (title, author, date, DOI) when the user clicks the extension icon. This permission limits access to only the active tab and only when the user clicks — no background access.

scripting:
Required to inject the metadata extraction content script into the active tab. The script reads structured data (meta tags, JSON-LD, Dublin Core) from the page to build the citation. Only injected on user action (clicking the icon or using the context menu).

storage:
Stores the user's citation library, projects, tags, and preferences locally on the device using chrome.storage.local. No data is transmitted to any server.

sidePanel:
Displays the library management panel alongside the browser window. Users can browse, search, edit, import, and export their citations in the side panel while continuing to research.

contextMenus:
Adds right-click options for citation workflows:
  - "Cite this page" — extract metadata from the current page
  - "Cite linked page" — resolve a DOI or ISBN link to full metadata
  - "Look up selected DOI/ISBN" — resolve selected identifier text
  - "Import DOIs from selection" — bulk import multiple DOIs from selected text

Host permissions (optional_host_permissions — requested at runtime, not at install):

https://arxiv.org/* and https://export.arxiv.org/*:
Fetches complete author lists and paper metadata from arXiv abstract pages. Academic papers on arXiv often lack metadata in their PDF files. This allows Ibid to fetch the HTML abstract page which contains Highwire citation meta tags with accurate author, title, and date information. Only the arXiv paper ID is used. Requested only when the user cites an arXiv paper.

https://api.semanticscholar.org/*:
Fallback metadata source for arXiv papers when the arXiv abstract page is unavailable. Semantic Scholar provides structured author and citation data. Only the arXiv paper ID is sent.

https://en.wikipedia.org/*:
Access to Wikipedia's Citoid citation service, which resolves any URL into structured citation metadata using Zotero translation servers. Only the page URL is sent, no personal data. Requested only when the user clicks Enhance with a URL.

https://doi.org/*:
Follows DOI redirect links to publisher article pages. Many PDFs contain DOIs that resolve to publisher pages with rich metadata. This permission allows Ibid to fetch that page and extract complete citation fields. Only the DOI is used.

Content Security Policy justification:
The extension uses wasm-unsafe-eval in its Content Security Policy. This is required to execute the Rust/WebAssembly citation engine (Hayagriva). It does not enable arbitrary JavaScript execution — only compiled WASM modules bundled with the extension.

ADDITIONAL NOTES FOR REVIEWER
------------------------------

- No <all_urls>, tabs, webRequest, webNavigation, identity, or other broad permissions
- Optional host permissions are NEVER granted automatically — the user is prompted at runtime
- If optional permissions are denied, the extension falls back to CORS-friendly APIs (OpenAlex, CrossRef, Open Library, Google Books, NCBI)
- No background network activity — all requests triggered by user action only
- No remote code execution — all JavaScript and WASM are bundled with the extension
- Source code: https://github.com/oriclabs/ibid
